content: HTA process follows standardized steps: (1) Scope definition - identify population, comparators, outcomes; (2) Systematic evidence review - clinical trials, observational studies; (3) Economic modeling - decision trees, Markov models projecting lifetime costs/outcomes; (4) Uncertainty analysis - sensitivity analyses, value of information; (5) Stakeholder consultation - patients, clinicians, manufacturers; (6) Appraisal committee decision. NICE's methods require intention-to-treat analysis, appropriate comparators (current standard care), and NHS perspective for costs. For end-of-life treatments, higher thresholds may apply. Managed entry agreements (price discounts, outcome-based payments) enable access when uncertainty is high.
